topic.title: Deprecation of Spice and Migration to VNC in &productname; 16

important.title: Removal of Spice in &suselinux; 16
important.para: Spice support for VMs has been removed beginning with SLE16. Attempting to start Spice based VMs on SLE16 will result in an error. Existing VMs created on SLE15-SP7 or older that use Spice must be converted to use VNC if run on SLE16. The virt-xml tool can be used to do this automatically beginning with virt-manager version 5.0.0.

section.title: Automatic conversion with virt-xml
section.para: To automatically convert a VM using virt-xml (virt-manager 5.0.0 or newer), follow these steps:

step.para: First backup your VM XML in case you do not like what virt-xml does.

listitem.para: The name of the VM. See virsh list --all on the host.
Convert an existing VM to exclusively use a single VNC graphics device.
Used with --convert-to-vnc to add a qemu-vdagent device if one is not already configured. This is for copy and paste functionality between host and guest on SLE16.

step.para: See the virt-xml man page for more options and details.

section.title: Manual conversion
section.para: If you prefer manual conversion, follow the step-by-step guide below.

step.para: Save the current XML definition for your spice based VM.
Optionally save a copy of the original spice based definition in case you mess something up.
Using your favorite text editor, proceed to edit the XML definition to remove or modify the spice based devices as follows.

section.title: Spice elements to remove
section.para: The following XML snippets are specific to the Spice protocol and must be removed from the VM's configuration file. These elements enable features like the Spice agent channel and USB redirection over Spice, which are no longer supported.
section.programlisting:
<!-- This channel is for the Spice agent and is no longer needed --> <channel type='spicevmc'> <target type='virtio' name='com.redhat.spice.0'/> <address type='virtio-serial' controller='0' bus='0' port='2'/> </channel>
<!-- These elements enable USB redirection over Spice and must be removed --> <redirdev bus='usb' type='spicevmc'> <address type='usb' bus='0' port='2'/> </redirdev>
<redirdev bus='usb' type='spicevmc'> <address type='usb' bus='0' port='3'/> </redirdev>

section.title: QXL Video Device Conversion for Windows VMs
section.para: The QXL video driver is optimized for the Spice protocol and is commonly used in Windows VMs. For better compatibility with VNC, we recommend replacing it with the more generic virtio video driver.
If your VM, especially a Windows guest, uses a QXL video device like this:
section.programlisting: <video> <model type='qxl' ram='65536' vram='65536' vgamem='16384' heads='1' primary='yes'/> <address type='pci' domain='0x0000' bus='0x00' slot='0x01' function='0x0'/> </video>
section.para: Replace the entire <video> block with a simpler virtio model definition:

note.para: You could specify more details about the above virtio video device, but libvirt will fill in any missing details.

section.title: Applying the new configuration and starting the VM

step.para: Redefine the guest VM based on the newly edited XML definition.
Start the VNC based guest VM.

section.title: Recommended for Copy and Paste functionality on VNC
section.para: With the absence of Spice on SLE16, the copy and paste functionality between host and guest was lost. The following steps may be done to restore this capability.

step.para: Install updated gtk-vnc RPMs on the SLE16 host. These newer RPMs support copy and paste with VNC. The FCS versions did not.
RPMs to update: libgtk-vnc-2_0-0, typelib-1_0-GtkVnc-2_0, libgvnc-1_0-0
Enable spice-vdagentd in the running SLES guest VM.
If not installed, run zypper in spice-vdagent.
Add the channel device XML below to the guest VM XML definition. This may be done in virt-manager (Add Hardware → Channel → Device Type: QEMU vdagent) or manually as shown below.
step.programlisting: <channel type="qemu-vdagent"> <source> <clipboard copypaste="yes"/> </source> <target type="virtio" name="com.redhat.spice.0"/> <address type="virtio-serial" controller="0" bus="0" port="1"/> </channel>
